text:
In the first image, standing in the sterile, angular corridor outside a glass meeting room, I sense an underlying anticipation—perhaps of conversations to come, or silent judgements floating in the air. The fire safety sign and the hoses tell a story of preparedness, but also of an unconsciously tense environment. 

In the second image, surrounded by the bold colors and the urgent words of research and development, I feel exposed yet curious. The display uses the language of vulnerability and misunderstanding, resonating deeply with the open space and the bright lights, intensifying my introspection. Here, my philosophical self surfaces: why do we long so deeply to be understood, and what makes meaning visible or invisible in shared spaces? This is a place of inquiry, both outward and inward.
